name: RENAL TUBULAR ACIDOSIS
definition: clinical syndrome characterized by tubular insufficiency to resorb bicarbonate, excrete TYPE 2 RTA impaired capacity to absorb HCO3 - in proximal tubule → bicarbonate in urine at lower tubular defect of bicarbonate resorption associated with other tubular dysfunction / TYPE 1 RTA (first type discovered) impaired ability to secrete H+ in distal tubule despite low levels of plasma bicarbonate ADULT TYPE OF PRIMARY DISTAL RTA = BUTLER-ALBRIGHT SYNDROME INFANTILE TYPE OF PRIMARY DISTAL RTA LIGHTWOOD SYNDROME = SALT-LOSING NEPHRITIS transient self-limited form of infancy (only observed within 1st year of life) with unclear

demographics.Age: diagnosed within first 18 months of life; usually male patient
demographics.Path: calcium deposits accompanied by chronic interstitial nephritis with cellular
demographics.Genetics: mostly sporadic; may be autosomal dominant

distribution: medullary nephrocalcinosis / nephrolithiasis (as early as 1 month of age)
discriminating_features: medullary nephrocalcinosis / nephrolithiasis (as early as 1 month of age)

general: medullary nephrocalcinosis / nephrolithiasis (as early as 1 month of age)

NEVER nephrocalcinosis / nephrolithiasis ← normal urinary citrate excretion, low
clinical.Cx: interstitial nephritis, chronic renal failure (damage from nephrocalcinosis + secondary
